title: Demo v. Secretary, No. 00-0001 (Demo Court 2024)
passage: An agency examination that fails to address a credibly raised theory of secondary causation is inadequate as a matter of law. Reliance on such an examination requires remand for a new opinion that confronts the omitted theory.

title: Demo v. Secretary, No. 00-0002 (Demo Court 2023)
passage: The contributing-cause standard for secondary connection is satisfied when the service-connected condition is one of multiple causes; sole causation is not required. A medical opinion stating the condition is 'multifactorial' does not, without more, defeat the claim.

title: Demo v. Secretary, No. 00-0003 (Demo Court 2022)
passage: A long latency period between in-service exposure and onset of the claimed condition is not, standing alone, sufficient to defeat service connection where competent medical evidence describes a recognized delayed-onset mechanism.

title: Demo v. Secretary, No. 00-0004 (Demo Court 2024)
passage: Lay evidence is competent to establish observable symptoms and their continuity. The factfinder may not categorically reject lay testimony for lack of medical expertise where the matters described are within ordinary observation.
